+    "title": "lima: `panic: runtime/cgo: misuse of an invalid Handle` (`Code-Hex/vz/v3.changeStateOnObserver`)",
+    "description": "`panic: runtime/cgo: misuse of an invalid Handle` (`Code-Hex/vz/v3.changeStateOnObserver`). This issue affects 6+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify lima troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your lima installation:\n```bash\nlimactl --version\nlimactl list\n```\nLook for error: `runtime/cgo: misuse of an invalid Handle`"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review lima configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant lima configuration:\n```\n$ limactl start template://experimental/vz\n\n$ limactl stop vz\nINFO[0000] Sending SIGINT to hostagent process 47742    \nINFO[0000] Waiting for the host agent and the driver processes to shut down \nINFO[0000] [hostagent] 2023/02/24 17:59:43"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for `panic: runtime/cgo: misuse of an invalid Handle`…",
+        "description": "> Looks like this happens only on stopping a new instance\n\nYes, looks like that for me too. It also hangs for almost another 3 minutes after the `panic()` before exiting the process. This seems to be inside the `vz` package, so I don't think we could capture it and exit right away (maybe not worth\n```yaml\n$ limactl start template://experimental/vz\n\n$ limactl stop vz\nINFO[0000] Sending SIGINT to hostagent process 47742    \nINFO[0000] Waiting for the host agent and the driver processes to shut down \nINFO[0000] [hostagent] 2023/02/24 17:59:43 tcpproxy: for incoming conn 127.0.0.1:51498, error dialing \"192.168.5.15:22\": connect tcp 192.168.5.15:22: no route to host \nINFO[0000] [hostagent] 2023/02/24 17:59:53 tcpproxy: for incoming conn 127.0.0.1:51499, error dialing \"192.168.5.15:22\": connect tcp 192.168.5.15:22: connection was refused \nINFO[0000] [hostagent] Received SIGINT, shutting down the host\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm `panic: runtime/cgo: misuse of an invalid Handle`… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\nTest lima to confirm the issue is resolved.\nConfirm that `runtime/cgo: misuse of an invalid Handle` no longer appears."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> Looks like this happens only on stopping a new instance\n\nYes, looks like that for me too. It also hangs for almost another 3 minutes after the `panic()` before exiting the process. This seems to be inside the `vz` package, so I don't think we could capture it and exit right away (maybe not worth doing anyways).",

+        "$ limactl start template://experimental/vz\n\n$ limactl stop vz\nINFO[0000] Sending SIGINT to hostagent process 47742    \nINFO[0000] Waiting for the host agent and the driver processes to shut down \nINFO[0000] [hostagent] 2023/02/24 17:59:43 tcpproxy: for incoming conn 127.0.0.1:51498, error dialing \"192.168.5.15:22\": connect tcp 192.168.5.15:22: no route to host \nINFO[0000] [hostagent] 2023/02/24 17:59:53 tcpproxy: for incoming conn 127.0.0.1:51499, error dialing \"192.168.5.15:22\": connect tcp 192.168.5.15:22: connection was refused \nINFO[0000] [hostagent] Received SIGINT, shutting down the host agent \nINFO[0000] [hostagent] Shutting down the host agent     \nINFO[0000] [hostagent] Stopping forwarding \"/run/lima-guestagent.sock\" (guest) to \"/Users/suda/.lima/vz/ga.sock\" (host) \nINFO[0000] [ho",
